primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, dans les comptes consolidés, de l’éligibilité du risque de change sur un dividende intragroupe / une créance intragroupe comme élément couvert dans une relation de couverture

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by first identifying the single governing lineage for hedge-accounting eligibility; IAS 39 directly governs designation of hedged items and hedging instruments, whereas IAS 21 expressly excludes hedge accounting and only provides supporting foreign-currency consequences.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low: IAS 39 is the clearest governing source for hedge-accounting designation, while IAS 21 remains relevant only to support whether intragroup foreign-currency exposures survive consolidation.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern which items may be designated in hedge accounting, including the consolidated-statements restriction for intragroup items and the foreign-currency exceptions.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify when exchange differences on intragroup monetary items remain in consolidated financial statements and therefore help assess the foreign-currency exposure that may underpin hedge designation.
This Interpretation supports the identification of net investment hedging as a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign-currency risk in consolidated financial statements.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address related-party disclosure and elimination of intragroup balances, but they do not identify peer hedge-accounting approaches.
These paragraphs distinguish separate from consolidated financial statements generally, but they do not govern hedge-accounting models for the issue raised.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for a recognised foreign-currency monetary item
Hedge accounting for variability in foreign-currency cash flows of forecast or firm-commitment exposures
Hedge accounting for foreign-currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 treats a recognised asset or liability, including a foreign-currency monetary item if eligible in consolidation, within the fair value hedge model.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 separately recognises cash flow hedging as a top-level hedge-accounting model for foreign-currency exposures such as forecast transactions or firm commitments.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify hedging of the foreign-currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge-accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
